o-que-e-cais-valongo:
The **Valongo Wharf** is the most important material vestige of the arrival of enslaved Africans in the Americas — and one of the most significant sites in the history of Brazil and of the African diaspora worldwide.

Built in **1811** in Rio's port area, it was the main landing point for enslaved Africans. It is estimated that between **500,000 and 1 million people** passed through here, coming mainly from West and Central Africa.

In **1843**, the wharf was renovated and buried to receive Empress Teresa Cristina — an act of erasing the memory of slavery. In **1911**, during the great urban reform, it was buried again.

Only in **2011**, during the Porto Maravilha works, did workers find the original stones 1 meter underground. Along with them: African amulets, cowrie shells and personal objects of those who landed there.

- **IPHAN:** heritage protection in 2013
- **UNESCO:** World Heritage Site on 9 July 2017
- **Law 15,203/2025:** recognized as Afro-Brazilian heritage essential to the national identity

The visit is **free**. It's on Rua Sacadura Cabral, in Saúde. The tram (VLT) stops at Harmonia station, a 5-minute walk away.
quando-vlt-inaugurado:
The **VLT Carioca** (tram) was inaugurated on **5 June 2016**, on the eve of the Rio Olympic Games, as part of the Porto Maravilha project.

Operated by Motiva (CCR Group), it now runs **4 lines** across Downtown and the port area:

- **Line 1 (Blue):** Santos Dumont → Terminal Gentileza
- **Line 2 (Green):** Praça XV → Praia Formosa
- **Line 3 (Yellow):** Santos Dumont → Central
- **Line 4 (Orange):** Praça XV → Terminal Gentileza

It connects the main train (Central), subway (Carioca, Cinelândia, Central) and ferry (Praça XV) stations, as well as the port area's sights.

**How to pay:** Jaé card or QR code via the Jaé app.

**Fare:** R$ 5.00 (subject to change).

**Hours:** every day, 5am to 11pm (subject to change).
familia-real-chegada-1808:
In **November 1807**, Napoleon Bonaparte invaded Portugal. Dom João VI (then Prince Regent, as his mother, Queen Maria I, was incapacitated) made an unprecedented decision: **to move the entire court to Brazil**.

The fleet of **36 ships** left Lisbon on 29 November 1807, with between **10,000 and 15,000 people** aboard — courtiers, officials, soldiers, aristocrats and clergy.

**The voyage:**
- First stop: Salvador, Bahia (22 January 1808) — where Dom João signed the decree opening Brazil's ports to friendly nations, breaking the Portuguese colonial monopoly
- Arrival in Rio de Janeiro: **7 March 1808**

**The impact on Rio:**
Rio became the capital of the Portuguese Empire — the only time in history a European metropolis was governed from its colony.

Dom João created: the Royal Library (1810, today the National Library), the Botanical Garden (1808), the Royal Press (1808), the Bank of Brazil (1808), the Royal Military Academy and the Anatomical, Surgical and Medical School.

The royal family settled in the **Paço Imperial** (Praça XV) and the Carmo Church.
o-que-e-porto-maravilha:
**Porto Maravilha** is the largest urban revitalization project in Rio de Janeiro's history — and one of the largest in Brazil.

Created by **Municipal Law 101 of 23/11/2009**, it is a Consortium Urban Operation that transformed **5 million m²** of the port area in the Saúde, Gamboa, Santo Cristo and Caju neighborhoods.

**Investment:** R$ 8 billion in a public-private partnership — the largest urban PPP in Brazil.

**What was done:**
- Demolition of 3.5 km of the Perimetral viaduct (which blocked the sea view)
- Construction of the tram (VLT) (opened June 2016)
- 17 km of bike lanes
- 700 km of renewed sewage networks
- Discovery and preservation of the Valongo Wharf (2011)
- Opening of the Museum of Tomorrow (2015), MAR (2013) and AquaRio (2016)
- Olympic Boulevard with international graffiti murals

The project was created to prepare Rio for the **2016 Olympic Games** and permanently transformed the city's relationship with its historic port.
museu-do-amanha-informacoes:
The **Museum of Tomorrow** is one of the most innovative science museums in the world. It opened on **17 December 2015** and was designed by the Spanish architect **Santiago Calatrava** — who also created the Milwaukee Art Museum (USA).

**Where:** Píer Mauá, Praça Mauá 1 — the structure reaches out over the waters of Guanabara Bay.

**What to see:**
The museum is 100% experiential and interactive — it has no traditional objects or collections. It explores 5 big themes:
1. Cosmos — the origin of the universe
2. Earth — the planet's history
3. Anthropocene — the era of human impact
4. Tomorrows — possible futures
5. The Human — what we are

**Tickets:**
- Full: R$ 30
- Half: R$ 15
- **Free on Wednesdays**

**Hours:** Tuesday to Sunday, 10am to 6pm (last entry 5pm). Closed on Mondays.

**How to get there:** Tram (VLT) — Museum of Tomorrow station (direct exit).
arcos-da-lapa-historia:
The **Lapa Arches** — official name: Carioca Aqueduct — were built between **1723 and 1750** to carry water from the Carioca River to the fountain at Largo do Paço (today's Praça XV).

**Dimensions:**
- 270 meters long
- 17.6 meters high
- 42 arches of stone and lime

They served as an aqueduct until **1896**, when they were adapted as an **electric tram viaduct** to the Santa Teresa neighborhood — a role they still play today. The famous **Santa Teresa tram** still crosses the Arches daily.

Over the 20th century, the area around the Arches became the heart of **carioca bohemia**. Lapa is famous for its samba circles, bars and nightlife. Historic figures such as the legendary **Madame Satã** (João Francisco dos Santos, 1900–1976) lived and became legend in this neighborhood.

**Tip:** on Thursday, Friday and Saturday nights Lapa fills with samba circles — a must-see experience.
escadaria-selaron-historia:
The **Selarón Steps** were created by the artist **Jorge Selarón** (1947–2013), born in Chile and settled in Rio de Janeiro.

Selarón began the project in **1990**, after moving into a house on Rua Joaquim Silva, in Lapa, with the deteriorated staircase in front. He started with scraps and broken tiles he found in the streets.

Over time, the work gained worldwide fame and people from all over the planet started sending tiles — today the staircase has pieces from **more than 60 countries**.

**Numbers:**
- 215 steps
- More than 60 countries represented in tiles
- Connects Lapa to the Santa Teresa neighborhood

Selarón said the work was *'my tribute to the Brazilian people'*. The most repeated motif is a pregnant Black woman — according to him, a homage to Afro-Brazilian motherhood.

On **10 January 2013** — his 65th birthday — Selarón was found dead on the staircase, in circumstances never fully clarified.

**Visit:** free, 24 hours. It's between Lapa and Santa Teresa.
paco-imperial-historia:
The **Paço Imperial** is the most important historic building in Rio de Janeiro. Built between **1738 and 1743** by the Portuguese engineer José Fernandes Pinto Alpoim, it was the center of colonial, imperial and republican power in Brazil.

**Timeline:**
- **1603:** First simple building on the site
- **1738–1743:** Construction of the present palace (Governors' House)
- **1763:** Capital moves from Salvador to Rio → becomes the 'Palace of the Viceroys'
- **1808:** Dom João VI arrives → 'Royal Palace' — seat of the Portuguese monarchy in Brazil
- **1822:** Dom Pedro I proclaims Brazil's Independence (7 September)
- **1888:** Princess Isabel signs the **Golden Law** (13 May) — the end of slavery in Brazil
- **1889:** Proclamation of the Republic — the palace loses its function
- **1985:** Restored and reopened as a cultural center

**Today:** A cultural center with free visual-arts exhibitions, shows and events.

**How to get there:** Tram (VLT) Paço Imperial — door to door. It's at Praça XV de Novembro.
aquario-aquario-info:
**AquaRio** is the **largest aquarium in South America**, opened on **26 December 2016** in the Gamboa neighborhood, in Rio's port area.

**Numbers:**
- 8,000 m² of total area
- 28 tanks
- More than **8,000 animals** of **350+ species**
- Main tank: **3.5 million liters** of water

**Highlights:**
- A 7-meter underwater tunnel (you walk with fish all around)
- Tiger sharks and rays
- Sea turtles
- Rare species of Brazilian tropical fish

**Tickets:**
- R$ 100–150 (varies by season)
- Half price for students and seniors
- Children up to 2: free

**Hours:** Monday to Friday 9am–5pm / Saturday and Sunday 9am–6pm.

**How to get there:** Tram (VLT) Santos Dumont station (10 min walk). Address: Rua Gavião Peixoto, 83 — Gamboa.
real-gabinete-leitura-info:
The **Royal Portuguese Reading Room** is one of the most beautiful libraries in the world — as ranked by **National Geographic**.

Founded in **1837** by Portuguese immigrants in Rio de Janeiro, the present building opened on **30 October 1887**. The style is **Manueline** (16th-century Portuguese Gothic), the same as the Jerónimos Monastery in Lisbon.

**Architect:** Rafael da Silva e Castro (Portuguese).
**Collection:** more than **350,000 volumes**, including rare works from the 16th to 18th centuries — first editions of Camões's *The Lusiads*, works by Eça de Queirós and historical manuscripts.

It is one of the largest collections in the Portuguese language outside Portugal.

**Visit:** completely **free**. Just walk in and admire the central reading room with its glass ceiling, cast-iron structure and shelves rising to the ceiling.

**Hours:** Monday to Friday 9am–6pm / Saturday 9am–1pm.

**How to get there:** Carioca subway (200m) or Carioca tram (VLT) station. Address: Rua Luís de Camões, 30 — Downtown.
central-brasil-historia:
**Central do Brasil Station** is one of Rio de Janeiro's historic icons and the largest railway hub in Brazil.

**Timeline:**
- **1858:** The Dom Pedro II Railway begins operating — one of Brazil's first railways
- **1943:** The present building is inaugurated by President Getúlio Vargas — Art Deco style
- **Tower:** 135 meters tall, one of the highest points in Rio when built
- **Today:** More than **600,000 passengers/day** — SuperVia + subway

**In film:**
In **1998**, Walter Salles's film *Central Station*, starring **Fernanda Montenegro**, turned the station into a worldwide cinematic symbol. The film won the **Golden Bear at the Berlin Festival** and was nominated for the **Oscar for Best Foreign Film** — Fernanda Montenegro received an Oscar nomination for Best Actress.

**How to use it:**
SuperVia trains to the entire North Zone and Baixada Fluminense depart from Central. It's also an interchange with the **subway** (Lines 1 and 2) and the **tram (VLT)**.
cinelandia-teatro-municipal:
**Cinelândia** — officially Praça Floriano — is the historic cultural center of Rio de Janeiro. It brings together four monuments around a square:

**Municipal Theater (1909)**
- Opened on 14 July 1909
- Inspired by the Paris Opera (Palais Garnier)
- Capacity: 2,357 seats
- Permanent program of opera, ballet and concerts

**National Library (1910)**
- Founded by **Dom João VI in 1810** (he brought 60,000 books from Lisbon while fleeing Napoleon)
- Largest library in **Latin America**: 9+ million items

**National Museum of Fine Arts (1938)**
- Collection with works by Víctor Meirelles and Pedro Américo
- Free admission on Saturdays

**The name 'Cinelândia'**
Comes from the 1920s, when several luxury cinemas opened on the square: Cine Odeon, Cine Pathé, Cine Glória. Today almost all have been demolished, but the **Cine Odeon** survived as an art-house cinema.

**How to get there:** Cinelândia subway station (direct exit to the square).
pedra-do-sal-samba:
**Carioca samba** has its roots in the Saúde neighborhood, in the port area — especially at the **Pedra do Sal**.

In the early 20th century, Saúde concentrated Rio's largest free African and Afro-Brazilian population — called **'Little Africa'** by the sambista Heitor dos Prazeres.

Around the Pedra do Sal gathered the pioneers of samba:
- **Donga** (Ernesto dos Santos) — one of the creators of recorded samba
- **João da Baiana** — percussionist and composer
- **Hilário Jovino Ferreira** — founder of carnival blocks

The **first official samba recording** — *'Pelo Telefone'* (1917) — is linked to this cultural circle.

The Pedra was **listed as cultural heritage in 1984**.

**Today:** every Monday and Friday night there are **samba circles** around the Pedra — one of the most authentic experiences Rio offers. Free entry.

**How to get there:** Harmonia tram (VLT) station (12 min walk). Largo João da Baiana, Saúde.
confeitaria-colombo-info:
The **Confeitaria Colombo** is one of Brazil's greatest gastronomic landmarks. Founded on **17 November 1894** by Portuguese immigrants, it is one of the oldest patisseries in the world still operating in the same location.

**The interior:**
Remodeled in 1913 in **Art Nouveau** style, with enormous Belgian crystal mirrors (brought from Belgium), solid jacaranda wood and colored stained glass. One of the most beautiful interiors in Rio.

**History:**
During the carioca Belle Époque (1900–1930), it was the meeting point of writers such as **Olavo Bilac** and **Machado de Assis**, politicians and artists. President Getúlio Vargas was a regular.

**What to order:** the cakes and sweets are the best in the area.

**Hours:** Monday to Friday 9am–8pm / Saturday 9am–6pm.

**Address:** Rua Gonçalves Dias, 32 — Downtown.

**How to get there:** Carioca subway or tram (VLT) (3 min walk).

**To eat in the area (traditional carioca cuisine):** Downtown and the port area keep the **botequim** culture — cold beer and draft with snacks like cod fritters, pastel and potatoes. **Feijoada** is the dish most associated with Rio, traditionally served on Saturdays; coffee with pão de queijo and street-fair pastel are also classics. Downtown and Saúde have old botequins; Praça Mauá and the Olympic Boulevard have more varied options with a sea view.

**Tip:** we don't list specific addresses here because menus, prices and hours change often — confirm on site or in a maps app before going.
roteiro-um-dia:
**A 1-day itinerary in Rio's port area and Downtown:**

**Morning (8am–12pm)**
- 8am: Paço Imperial (free) — Brazil's colonial history
- 9am: Praça XV and the Church of Our Lady of Mount Carmel
- 10am: Tram (VLT) to Parada dos Museus (Museum of Tomorrow area)
- 10:30am: **Museum of Tomorrow** (2 hours)

**Lunch (12pm–2pm)**
- A restaurant at Praça Mauá or on the Olympic Boulevard

**Afternoon (2pm–6pm)**
- 2pm: MAR — Rio Art Museum (half an hour on the terrace, free)
- 3pm: Harmonia tram (VLT) station → **Valongo Wharf** (free, 30 min)
- 4pm: **Pedra do Sal** — a walk through the Saúde neighborhood
- 5pm: Royal Portuguese Reading Room (free)

**Night (from 7pm)**
- **Lapa Arches** → Selarón Steps → live samba in Lapa

**Tip:** use the tram (VLT) to get around — pay with the Jaé card (fare R$ 5.00, subject to change).

🍽️ **To eat:** at Largo da Prainha (Rua Sacadura Cabral) are Angu do Gomes, Dois de Fevereiro and Gracioso; at Praça Mauá, Restaurante Mauá (MAR rooftop) and Boteco Belmonte. Ask *“where to eat in the port area”* for more options.
como-funciona-vlt:
The **VLT Carioca** (light rail / tram) is Rio's modern tram, opened in 2016 and operated by Motiva (CCR Group).

**4 lines:**
- **Line 1 (Blue):** Santos Dumont ↔ Terminal Gentileza
- **Line 2 (Green):** Praça XV ↔ Praia Formosa
- **Line 3 (Yellow):** Santos Dumont ↔ Central
- **Line 4 (Orange):** Praça XV ↔ Terminal Gentileza

**How to pay:**
- **Jaé card** — buy at the station machines or via the app, and top up via app/website/machines
- **QR code** generated by the Jaé app (a new one each trip)

**Fare:** R$ 5.00 (subject to change — confirm at the stations)

**Hours:** every day, 5am to 11pm (subject to change)

**Integration:** Bilhete Único Carioca (2h30 with city buses) and Bilhete Único Intermunicipal (3h with trains, ferries and vans). Between VLT lines, one fare within 1h. Connects with the subway and SuperVia at Central, and with ferries at Praça XV.

**Accessibility:** all stations are accessible for people with disabilities and reduced mobility.

**Tip:** the tram passes the port area's main sights — use it as a guide!
precos-horarios-gratuidade:
Many spots in the port area and Downtown are **free** — the **Valongo Wharf**, the **Olympic Boulevard**, the **Pedra do Sal**, the **Selarón Steps** and the historic churches all have free entry.

Among the paid ones, the **Museum of Tomorrow** costs about R$ 30 (half R$ 15) and is **free on Wednesdays**; the **AquaRio** is paid. (Prices subject to change.)

**Half price / free entry:** by law, students, people aged 60+, children and people with disabilities are entitled to half price or free entry — bring supporting ID.

**Hours:** most museums open Tuesday to Sunday and close on Mondays.

**Important:** prices and hours change often — always confirm on each attraction's official website before going.

**Tip:** for a place's exact price or hours, ask directly, e.g.: *“how much is the Museum of Tomorrow”* or *“what time does AquaRio open”*.
seguranca-dicas-praticas:
**Safety and practical tips for the port area:**

- Prefer to visit **during the day** and stick to busy areas — Praça Mauá, the Olympic Boulevard and around the museums.
- Use the **tram (VLT)** to move between attractions; at night, avoid isolated streets.
- Carry **little cash** and avoid showing your phone, camera or valuables.
- Keep an **ID** and bring a **card** — not every small place accepts electronic payment.
- **Restrooms** are in the museums and cultural centers; **public wi-fi** is limited — don't rely on it.
- **Accessibility:** the tram (VLT) and the main museums are accessible for people with disabilities and reduced mobility.
- **Emergencies:** Police **190** · Ambulance (SAMU) **192**.

ℹ️ **Note:** these are general guidelines and local conditions (safety, facilities and services) may change over time. Confirm up-to-date information before going and, if in doubt, look for a security officer or official signage on site.

**Tip:** the best time to explore the area is in the morning or late afternoon — there's more movement and the light is great for photos.
gamboa-acao:
**Gamboa Ação** is a civil-society organization (CSO) founded in **2014**, in Rio's port area, that serves children and teenagers in situations of social vulnerability in **Gamboa** and the **Providência** community.

Its purpose is to *develop free, productive, socially integrated and morally responsible individuals*, with holistic education in three areas: **education, art & culture, and sport & leisure** — plus healthy meals and quality time every day.

**How to help:** the organization welcomes volunteers, donations and partnerships.

📍 Rua Pedro Ernesto, 31 — Gamboa · 🕐 Mon–Fri, 2pm–6pm
📞 (21) 98899-3699 (WhatsApp) · ✉️ contato@gamboaacao.org.br · 📷 @gamboaacao · 🌐 gamboaacao.org.br

🚉 Nearest tram (VLT) station: **Providência** (Line 1).

**Tip:** it's not a tourist site — to learn about the work, volunteer or donate, reach out through the official channels (WhatsApp or Instagram).
praia-formosa-info:
**Praia Formosa** was once a real beach in the port area, in **Santo Cristo** — but it was **filled in from 1879**, during the works of the **Mangue Canal** and the **Port of Rio**. Today there is **no sea or sand**: the name now belongs to a tram (VLT) station.

🚊 The **Praia Formosa station** is the **terminus of Line 2 (Green)**, which links Praia Formosa to **Praça XV** (passing Central, SAARA and Cinelândia). It opened on **3 December 2017**.

📍 It's on Rua General Luís Mendes de Morais, next to the **Padre Henrique Otte bus terminal** and a few minutes from the **Novo Rio bus terminal** — the ideal station for those arriving by interstate bus who want to head Downtown by tram.

💲 It's a **public area**, no admission. The tram runs every day, ~5am to 11pm (subject to change).

ℹ️ **Important:** it's not a leisure beach — it's a historic reference and a transport point.
igreja-santa-rita-info:
⛪ The **Church of Santa Rita** is one of the oldest in Rio: construction began in **1721** and its parish, created in **1751**, was the **first in the Americas dedicated to Saint Rita**. In Baroque-Rococo style, it has been **listed by IPHAN** since 1938.

🕯️ Largo de Santa Rita holds a strong memory: before the Valongo Wharf, a cemetery of enslaved Africans (the 'pretos novos') existed there between 1722 and 1769 — a history recalled by the Santa Rita–Pretos Novos tram station.

📍 Free entry; confirm mass and visiting times on site. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Santa Rita–Pretos Novos** tram (VLT) station (Line 3) or **Candelária** (Lines 1 and 3), Downtown.
igreja-sao-francisco-penitencia-info:
⛪ The **Church of the Third Order of São Francisco da Penitência**, atop the **Largo da Carioca**, is considered the **greatest treasure of the Baroque in Rio**. Built up to **1748**, its interior is **entirely covered in gilded woodwork** (gold), with works by Francisco Xavier de Brito and Manuel de Brito.

✨ It sits next to the **Convent of Santo Antônio**, one of the city's oldest colonial ensembles — worth seeing both together.

📍 Confirm visiting times on site. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Carioca** subway/tram (VLT) station (VLT Lines 1 and 3), going up from the Largo da Carioca.
palacio-tiradentes-info:
🏛️ **Tiradentes Palace**, next to Praça XV, is the seat of the **ALERJ** (Rio State Assembly). The present eclectic building is from **1926**, erected where the old **Cadeia Velha (jail)** once stood — the place where **Tiradentes was imprisoned** before being executed in 1792, which gives the palace its name.

🖼️ It has a **permanent exhibition** on the history of the Brazilian parliament and a **free guided tour**.

📍 Confirm tour times on the official website. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Praça XV** tram (VLT) station (Lines 2 and 4), next to the Paço Imperial.
ilha-fiscal-info:
🏰 **Fiscal Island** is a **neo-Gothic castle from 1889** on an island in Guanabara Bay. It was the stage of the **Empire's Last Ball**, on **9 November 1889**, six days before the Proclamation of the Republic.

⚓ Today it is kept by the **Brazilian Navy** and visited on a **guided boat tour** departing from the Navy Cultural Space.

📍 Tours have set days/times and are usually paid — confirm before going. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Praça XV** tram (VLT) station (Lines 2 and 4); from there, walk to the Navy Cultural Space.
gare-leopoldina-info:
🚂 The **Gare Leopoldina** (Barão de Mauá Station) opened in **1926** and honors the **Baron of Mauá**, a railway pioneer in Brazil. The building, in **English Palladian** style with a large metal vault, was the origin of the **Trem de Prata** (Rio–São Paulo) in the 1990s.

🏛️ Closed to passengers in 2001, it is **listed** and today works as an events venue, in Santo Cristo.

📍 No regular tourist visits — check the schedule. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Rodoviária** tram (VLT) station (Lines 1, 2 and 4) or **Praia Formosa** (Line 2).
edificio-a-noite-info:
🏙️ The **A Noite Building**, at **Praça Mauá**, was **Latin America's first skyscraper** (opened in **1929**), with about 22 floors and 102 m in **Art Deco** style. Designed by Joseph Gire and Elisiário Bahiana, it housed the A Noite newspaper and the legendary **Rádio Nacional**.

🏛️ It has been **listed since 2013** and stands right across from the Museum of Tomorrow.

📍 No regular interior visits — admire the façade and take photos at Praça Mauá. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Parada dos Museus** tram (VLT) station (Line 1).
igreja-sao-jose-info:
⛪ The **Church of São José** originates from a **1608** hermitage and even served as the Mother Church and See of Rio. The present church was completed in **1842**, with **Rococo woodwork** by Simeão de Nazaré, a disciple of Mestre Valentim. It has been **listed by IPHAN** since 1938.

📍 It's a few steps from Praça XV and Tiradentes Palace; free entry — confirm mass and visiting times. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Praça XV** tram (VLT) station (Lines 2 and 4) or Cinelândia.
igreja-lapa-dos-mercadores-info:
⛪ The **Church of Our Lady of Lapa dos Mercadores**, on **Rua do Ouvidor**, is known as the **'Peddlers' Church'** — it was built by merchants from **1743/1747**. In Baroque-Rococo style, it has a rare **elliptical floor plan** with domes and lanterns, and was recently restored.

📍 Free entry; confirm mass and visiting times. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Candelária** tram (VLT) station (Lines 1 and 3) or **Praça XV** (Lines 2 and 4), Downtown.
cemiterio-dos-ingleses-info:
⚰️ The **English Cemetery** (British Burial Ground), in **Gamboa**, is the **oldest open-air cemetery in Brazil still in use**. It emerged in the early 19th century, after the arrival of the **Royal Family (1808)** and the opening of the ports, to bury British Protestants — barred from Catholic churches. The first burial was on **15 January 1811**.

🌿 Linked to the 1810 treaty between Portugal and England, it once had its own wharf on the old Saco da Gamboa.

📍 It's a private cemetery — visits usually require an appointment; confirm beforehand. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Gamboa** tram (VLT) station (Lines 1, 2 and 4) or **Providência** (Line 1).
praca-da-harmonia-info:
🌳 The **Praça da Harmonia** (officially **Praça Coronel Assunção**), in **Gamboa**, is one of the most historic squares in the port area. It emerged after the Pereira Passos redevelopment, on the site of the former Harmonia Market, and has a bust of Colonel Assunção, from 1959.

⚽ Fun fact: it was here that **Club de Regatas Vasco da Gama was founded, on 21 August 1898**. It's in the heart of Little Africa, near the Pedra do Sal and the Valongo Wharf.

📍 An open, free public square. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Parada Harmonia** tram (VLT) station (Line 1).
praca-tiradentes-info:
🏛️ **Praça Tiradentes**, Downtown, is one of the oldest squares in Rio — it has been Rossio Grande, Campo dos Ciganos and Campo da Lampadosa, and took its current name in **1890**, recalling Tiradentes, executed nearby in 1792.

🐎 At its center stands the **equestrian monument to Dom Pedro I** (1862), often mistaken for a statue of Tiradentes. Next to it is the historic João Caetano Theater.

📍 A free public square. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Tiradentes** tram (VLT) station (Lines 2 and 4).
praca-xv-info:
🏛️ **Praça XV** (Praça Quinze de Novembro) is the historic heart of Rio — the former **Terreiro do Paço**, the center of colonial and imperial power. It brings together the **Paço Imperial**, **Tiradentes Palace**, the **Teles Arch** and the **Mestre Valentim Fountain** (1789).

⛴️ The **ferries to Niterói** depart from here, with a beautiful Guanabara Bay view.

📍 A free public square. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Praça XV** tram (VLT) station (Lines 2 and 4).
largo-da-carioca-info:
⛪ The **Largo da Carioca**, Downtown, sits at the foot of the Santo Antônio Hill. On top are the **Convent of Santo Antônio** (1608) and the **Penitência Church**, a Baroque jewel — worth the climb.

🚶 It's one of the busiest spots Downtown, by the Carioca station.

📍 A free public square. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Carioca** subway/tram (VLT) station (VLT Lines 1 and 3).
largo-da-candelaria-info:
⛪ The **Largo da Candelária**, Downtown, faces the imposing **Candelária Church** (1898). It was the stage of historic moments, such as the **'Diretas Já'** rally (1984), and holds the **memorial to the victims of the Candelária Massacre** (1993).

🏛️ It's in the Downtown cultural block, near the CCBB and the Casa França-Brasil.

📍 A free public square. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Candelária** tram (VLT) station (Lines 1 and 3).
praca-da-cruz-vermelha-info:
🌳 The **Praça da Cruz Vermelha**, Downtown, emerged over the former **Morro do Senado** (leveled between 1880 and 1906), during the **Pereira Passos** reforms. The name comes from the seat of the **Brazilian Red Cross** (1923 building), across from it.

🚦 It's an important junction of avenues such as Mem de Sá and Henrique Valadares, near Lapa.

📍 A free public square. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Carioca** or Cinelândia subway/tram (VLT) station (a few blocks away).
passeio-publico-info:
🌳 The **Passeio Público** is the **first public park in Brazil**, opened in **1783** to a design by **Mestre Valentim**, over the landfill of the former Boqueirão Lagoon. It has tree-lined paths, fountains and historic bronze sculptures.

🏛️ It's a protected historic garden, between Lapa and Cinelândia.

📍 A gated park — confirm the hours before going; free entry. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Cinelândia** subway/tram (VLT) station.
praca-cardeal-camara-info:
🌉 The **Praça Cardeal Câmara**, Downtown, sits next to the **Lapa Arches** (the Carioca Aqueduct, from 1723), between Rua Riachuelo and Rua Frei Caneca. It's a passage between Downtown, Lapa and the climb to Santa Teresa.

🚋 The Santa Teresa tram, which crosses the Arches, runs through here.

📍 A free public square. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Carioca** or Cinelândia subway/tram (VLT) station.
campo-de-santana-info:
🌳 The **Campo de Santana** (Praça da República), Downtown, was where **Deodoro proclaimed the Republic, on 15 November 1889**. It has had several names (Campo da Aclamação, de Honra, da Liberdade).

🦫 Today it is a leafy park with a lake, an artificial grotto and free-roaming **agoutis** — great for a break near Central do Brasil.

📍 A gated park — confirm the hours; free entry. 🚊 **How to get there:** **Central** tram (VLT) station (Lines 2, 3 and 4); Central subway.
praca-paris-info:
⛲ **Praça Paris**, in **Glória**, is a **French-style garden** inspired by Versailles, designed by **Alfred Agache** (the Agache Plan) and opened in **1929**, over the Glória landfill. It has a monumental fountain, reflecting pools and tree-lined avenues.

📍 It's outside the port area; a free public square. 🚇 **How to get there:** **Glória subway** (Line 1) — **there is no tram (VLT)** nearby.
praca-da-bandeira-info:
🚩 The **Praça da Bandeira** gives its name to the neighborhood, where the north, south and central zones meet. It was once the **Praça do Matadouro** (a municipal slaughterhouse until 1881) and its name is linked to the **raising of the Brazilian flag (1889)**.

📍 It's outside the port area and is a major road junction. 🚆 **How to get there:** by trains/buses on the Radial Oeste — **there is no tram (VLT)** nearby.
praca-sao-salvador-info:
🎶 **Praça São Salvador**, on the border of **Flamengo** and Laranjeiras, is a carioca bohemian spot: it has a bandstand, a playground and **open-air music shows** on weekend nights, surrounded by bars.

📍 It's outside the port area; a free public square. 🚇 **How to get there:** **Largo do Machado** or Catete subway (Lines 1 and 2) — **there is no tram (VLT)** nearby.
praca-onze-info:
🥁 **Praça Onze de Junho** (Cidade Nova) was one of the **cradles of samba and carnival** in Rio, in the heart of **Little Africa**: the first samba school (**Deixa Falar**, 1928) emerged here and the Bahian 'aunts', such as **Tia Ciata**, gathered here. The name recalls the Battle of Riachuelo (1865).

🎭 It's near the Sambódromo and the Cidade do Samba.

📍 A free public square. 🚇 **How to get there:** **Praça Onze** subway (Line 2); **Central** tram (VLT) (Lines 2, 3 and 4) a few blocks away.
praca-saens-pena-info:
🛍️ **Praça Saens Peña** is the commercial heart of **Tijuca**. It was the former **Largo da Fábrica das Chitas** and was renamed in **1911**, in honor of the former Argentine presidents **Sáenz Peña**.

📍 It's outside the port area, in the North Zone; a free public square. 🚇 **How to get there:** **Saens Peña / Tijuca** subway (Line 1) — **there is no tram (VLT)** nearby.
estacao-santos-dumont:
🚊 **Santos Dumont** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 3 (Yellow), in Downtown.

The tram terminal at Santos Dumont Airport — direct connection to domestic flights. Served by Lines 1 (Blue) and 3 (Yellow).

📌 Nearby: Santos Dumont Airport (~100 m) · AquaRio (~900 m) · National Historical Museum (~600 m).

ℹ️ Direct connection to Santos Dumont Airport. Ideal for reaching Downtown after a domestic flight.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-antonio-carlos-mam:
🚊 **Antônio Carlos - MAM** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 3 (Yellow), in Downtown.

Near the Museum of Modern Art and Santos Dumont Airport. Served by Lines 1 (Blue) and 3 (Yellow).

📌 Nearby: Museum of Modern Art (MAM) (~300 m) · Santos Dumont Airport (~500 m) · National Historical Museum (~400 m).

ℹ️ A good option for the MAM or Santos Dumont Airport.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-cinelandia:
🚊 **Cinelândia** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 3 (Yellow), in Downtown.

Rio's cultural heart — the Municipal Theater, National Library and National Museum of Fine Arts. Served by Lines 1 (Blue) and 3 (Yellow).

📌 Nearby: Municipal Theater (~100 m) · National Library (~150 m) · National Museum of Fine Arts (~200 m).

ℹ️ Interchange with Subway Line 1 (Cinelândia station). The richest cultural station on the tram.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-carioca:
🚊 **Carioca** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 3 (Yellow), in Downtown.

At the Largo da Carioca — interchange with Subway Line 1. Served by Lines 1 (Blue) and 3 (Yellow).

📌 Nearby: Royal Portuguese Reading Room (~200 m) · Confeitaria Colombo (~400 m) · Lapa Arches (~700 m).

ℹ️ Interchange with Subway Line 1 (Carioca station). One of the most central and best-connected tram stations.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-sete-de-setembro:
🚊 **Sete de Setembro** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 3 (Yellow), in Downtown.

On Avenida Rio Branco, Downtown's main commercial and historic axis. Served by Lines 1 (Blue) and 3 (Yellow).

📌 Nearby: CCBB — Banco do Brasil Cultural Center (~200 m) · Confeitaria Colombo (~400 m) · Royal Portuguese Reading Room (~500 m).

ℹ️ A central station with easy access to several cultural attractions. The CCBB is a 3-minute walk away.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-candelaria:
🚊 **Candelária** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 3 (Yellow), in Downtown.

Near the Candelária Church and Avenida Rio Branco. Served by Lines 1 (Blue) and 3 (Yellow).

📌 Nearby: Candelária Church (~100 m) · Correios Cultural Center (~200 m) · Avenida Rio Branco (~100 m).

ℹ️ The Candelária Church is open every day — free entry. One of Rio's most photographed landmarks.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-sao-bento:
🚊 **São Bento** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), in Downtown.

Near the Monastery of São Bento, founded in 1590 — one of the most beautiful Baroque ensembles in Brazil. Served by Line 1 (Blue).

📌 Nearby: Monastery of São Bento (~200 m) · Praça Mauá (~500 m).

ℹ️ The Monastery of São Bento offers a sung Gregorian mass on Sundays at 10am — free entry, no booking.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-parada-dos-museus:
🚊 **Parada dos Museus** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), in Downtown / Porto Maravilha.

The stop serving the Praça Mauá museums area — the Museum of Tomorrow and MAR (Rio Art Museum). Served by Line 1 (Blue).

📌 Nearby: Museum of Tomorrow (~250 m) · MAR — Rio Art Museum (~150 m) · Praça Mauá (~100 m).

ℹ️ Exit for the Museum of Tomorrow, MAR and Praça Mauá. (Approximate coordinate for the museums area.)

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-parada-dos-navios-valongo:
🚊 **Parada dos Navios - Valongo** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), in Saúde.

The station honors the Valongo Wharf, the main port of entry for enslaved Africans in the Americas. Served by Line 1 (Blue).

📌 Nearby: Valongo Wharf (~100 m) · Largo do Valongo (~150 m) · Pretos Novos Institute (~400 m).

ℹ️ The most historic tram station. Direct exit to the Valongo Wharf — a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 9 July 2017.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-utopia-aquario:
🚊 **Utopia - AquaRio** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), in Santo Cristo.

Near AquaRio, the Armazém Utopia and the RioStar (Ferris wheel). Served by Line 1 (Blue).

📌 Nearby: AquaRio (~150 m) · RioStar (~200 m) · Armazém Utopia (~100 m).

ℹ️ The best station for AquaRio and the RioStar. Buy tickets in advance in high season.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-cidade-do-samba-tia-ciata:
🚊 **Cidade do Samba - Tia Ciata** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), in Gamboa / Santo Cristo.

Near the Cidade do Samba, the complex of warehouses of the Special Group samba schools. It honors Tia Ciata, a historic figure of carioca samba. Served by Line 1 (Blue).

ℹ️ Access to the Cidade do Samba, where the carnival floats and costumes are built. (Cidade do Samba coordinate.)

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-santo-cristo:
🚊 **Santo Cristo** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Santo Cristo.

In the Santo Cristo neighborhood, in the revitalized port area. Served by Lines 1 (Blue), 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

ℹ️ An interchange station between the three lines that serve the port area. (Approximate coordinate.)

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-cordeiro-da-graca:
🚊 **Cordeiro da Graça** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Santo Cristo.

On Rua Cordeiro da Graça, in Santo Cristo, along the Via Binário do Porto. Served by Lines 1 (Blue), 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

ℹ️ A through station on the Via Binário, in Santo Cristo. (Rua Cordeiro da Graça coordinate.)

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-harmonia:
🚊 **Harmonia** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), in Saúde.

In the heart of the historic port area, near the Morro da Conceição and the Valongo Wharf. Served by Line 1 (Blue).

📌 Nearby: Valongo Wharf (~400 m) · Morro da Conceição (~600 m) · Pedra do Sal (~700 m).

ℹ️ The best station for visiting the Valongo Wharf and the Morro da Conceição. The historic part of the port area.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-providencia:
🚊 **Providência** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), in Gamboa.

Near the cable car to the Morro da Providência, the first favela in Brazil. Served by Line 1 (Blue).

📌 Nearby: Morro da Providência (~200 m) · Providência Cable Car (~250 m).

ℹ️ The best station for visiting the Morro da Providência. The cable car runs at specific times — check beforehand.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-gamboa:
🚊 **Gamboa** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Gamboa.

In the Gamboa neighborhood, the cradle of Rio's port culture and near the cable car to the Morro da Providência. Served by Lines 1 (Blue), 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: Providência Cable Car (~400 m) · Valongo Wharf (~600 m).

ℹ️ The starting point to go up the Morro da Providência by cable car. A neighborhood with a strong cultural and historic identity.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-pereira-reis:
🚊 **Pereira Reis** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Santo Cristo.

A station on the Via Binário do Porto, the central artery of the revitalized port area. Served by Lines 1 (Blue), 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: AquaRio (~400 m) · RioStar (Ferris wheel) (~500 m).

ℹ️ A good station to access AquaRio and the RioStar, the port area's big entertainment attractions.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-equador:
🚊 **Equador** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Santo Cristo.

A station in Santo Cristo, near the Port of Rio. Served by Lines 1 (Blue), 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: Port of Rio de Janeiro (~300 m) · AquaRio (~500 m).

ℹ️ A station near the cruise terminal. A good option for those arriving in Rio by ship.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-rodoviaria:
🚊 **Rodoviária** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Santo Cristo.

The station for the Novo Rio bus terminal — arrivals by bus from all over Brazil and South America. Served by Lines 1 (Blue), 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: Novo Rio Bus Terminal (~50 m) · Valongo Wharf (~900 m) · AquaRio (~600 m).

ℹ️ Integration with city and interstate bus lines. The ideal starting point for those arriving in Rio by bus.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-terminal-intermodal-gentileza:
🚊 **Terminal Intermodal Gentileza** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 1 (Blue), Line 4 (Orange), in São Cristóvão.

An integration terminal with the BRT and city buses — a gateway to the port area. Served by Lines 1 (Blue) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: BRT Gentileza Terminal (~50 m) · Quinta da Boa Vista (~1500 m).

ℹ️ The main integration hub at the northern end of the tram. Connects with the BRT and buses across the city.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-praca-xv:
🚊 **Praça XV** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Downtown.

A tram terminal with connections to ferries to Niterói and Paquetá. A landmark of colonial Rio. Served by Lines 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: Ferry Terminal (~100 m) · Paço Imperial (~150 m) · Church of Our Lady of Mount Carmel (~200 m).

ℹ️ Connection to ferries to Niterói (every 30 min) and Paquetá. Rio's densest historic area — Paço Imperial, Praça XV and colonial churches.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-colombo:
🚊 **Colombo** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Downtown.

It honors the Confeitaria Colombo — Rio's most famous historic patisserie, open since 1894. Served by Lines 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: Confeitaria Colombo (~150 m) · Royal Portuguese Reading Room (~250 m) · CCBB (~400 m).

ℹ️ The Confeitaria Colombo is a must — coffee and cakes in a historic setting. Lunch is pricier; breakfast and afternoon tea are the best options.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-tiradentes:
🚊 **Tiradentes** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Downtown.

At Praça Tiradentes — Rio's historic theater hub, with Tiradentes Palace. Served by Lines 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: Tiradentes Palace (~100 m) · Royal Portuguese Reading Room (~400 m) · Carlos Gomes Theater (~150 m).

ℹ️ Tiradentes Palace offers free guided tours. Praça Tiradentes is the historic center of carioca theater.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-saara:
🚊 **Saara** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Downtown.

At SAARA — the largest popular shopping hub in Rio. Served by Lines 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: SAARA (~50 m) · Church of São Francisco de Paula (~300 m) · Praça Tiradentes (~200 m).

ℹ️ The heart of Downtown's popular shopping. SAARA is famous for low prices and cultural diversity — Armenians, Arabs, Jews and Brazilians have coexisted there for decades.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-cristiano-ottoni-pequena-africa:
🚊 **Cristiano Ottoni - Pequena África** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 2 (Green), Line 3 (Yellow), Line 4 (Orange), in Downtown.

It honors Little Africa — the historic territory of Afro-Brazilian culture in Rio. Served by Lines 2 (Green), 3 (Yellow) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: Pedra do Sal (~600 m) · Valongo Wharf (~500 m) · Pretos Novos Institute (~400 m).

ℹ️ An access point to Rio's Afro-Brazilian history. The name honors the engineer Cristiano Ottoni and Little Africa, the Black cultural territory.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-central:
🚊 **Central** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 2 (Green), Line 3 (Yellow), Line 4 (Orange), in Downtown.

Rio's most important transport hub — tram, subway and SuperVia trains converge here (Central do Brasil Station). Served by Lines 2 (Green), 3 (Yellow) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: Central do Brasil Station (~50 m) · Praça da República (~200 m) · Campo de Santana (~300 m).

ℹ️ Rio's main transport hub. Interchange with the subway (Lines 1 and 2) and SuperVia across the city. The Central clock tower is a historic meeting point.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-vila-olimpica:
🚊 **Vila Olímpica** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 2 (Green), Line 4 (Orange), in Santo Cristo.

Near the tram's Operations Center and the old port workers' village. Served by Lines 2 (Green) and 4 (Orange).

📌 Nearby: Novo Rio Bus Terminal (~600 m) · AquaRio (~700 m).

ℹ️ A through station between the bus terminal and Downtown. The Santo Cristo neighborhood is undergoing urban transformation.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-praia-formosa:
🚊 **Praia Formosa** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 2 (Green), in Santo Cristo.

The origin terminal of Line 2, near the Padre Henrique Otte bus terminal. Served by Line 2 (Green).

📌 Nearby: Praia Formosa (memory of the vanished beach) (~0 m) · Padre Henrique Otte Bus Terminal (~100 m) · Novo Rio Bus Terminal (~800 m).

ℹ️ The start of tram Line 2. Good integration with buses to the North Zone.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-santa-rita-pretos-novos:
🚊 **Santa Rita - Pretos Novos** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 3 (Yellow), in Downtown.

It honors the Pretos Novos Institute — archaeology and memory of the trade in enslaved Africans. Served by Line 3 (Yellow).

📌 Nearby: Pretos Novos Institute (~300 m) · Church of Santa Rita (~150 m).

ℹ️ The Pretos Novos Institute is one of the most important places to understand the history of slavery in Rio de Janeiro.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
estacao-camerino-rosas-negras:
🚊 **Camerino - Rosas Negras** is a VLT Carioca (tram) station — Line 3 (Yellow), in Downtown.

It honors the Rosas Negras club, an important Black carioca club of the 20th century. Served by Line 3 (Yellow).

📌 Nearby: Federal Justice Cultural Center (~300 m) · Church of Santa Rita (~200 m).

ℹ️ A station with strong significance for Afro-carioca culture. In the colonial historic Downtown.

💡 Pay with the Jaé card or a QR code via the Jaé app (fare subject to change).
gastronomia-regiao-portuaria:
🍽️ The port area is a food hub full of history! Some highlights:

🥘 **Largo da Prainha (Rua Sacadura Cabral, Saúde)** — gathers the classics: **Angu do Gomes** (since 1955), **Dois de Fevereiro** (Bahian, by chef João Diamante), **G&G Gourmet** (bobó), **Gracioso** and **Boteco Pedra do Sal** (samba and snacks).
🌇 **Praça Mauá** — **Restaurante Mauá** (MAR rooftop, with a view) and **Boteco Belmonte** (Touring Building).
⛰️ **Morro da Conceição** — **Imaculada** and **Café Tero**.
☕ Downtown, the historic **Confeitaria Colombo** (1894).

🚊 Almost everything is a few minutes from the **Parada dos Navios–Valongo** and **Parada dos Museus** stations (Line 1).
💡 Tip: Rua Sacadura Cabral has several places in a row — you can visit more than one on the same outing! (Regional bonus: the traditional **São Cristóvão Fair**, a stronghold of northeastern food, is right near the port area.)
botecos-regiao-portuaria:
🍺 The best botequins (bars) in the port area:

🍻 **Gracioso** (Rua Sacadura Cabral, 97 – Saúde) — a traditional dive bar since 1960, feijoada on Thursdays and a samba circle.
🐟 **Beco das Sardinhas / Bar Ocidental** (Rua Miguel Couto, 139 – Downtown) — freshly fried sardines, snacks and draft on the street.
🥁 **Boteco Pedra do Sal** (Sacadura Cabral, 120) — at the cradle of samba, snacks in the late afternoon.
🍢 **Ladainha** (Sacadura Cabral, 57) and **Boteco Belmonte** (Touring Building, Praça Mauá, with a view).
🌅 **Bar do Omar** (Morro do Pinto) — a bar with a view of the bay and Christ the Redeemer.

🚊 How to get there: Parada dos Navios–Valongo and Parada dos Museus stations (Line 1).
💡 Tip: Rua Sacadura Cabral has several bars in a row — perfect for a snack crawl!
frutos-do-mar-regiao-portuaria:
🐟 Seafood and Bahian cuisine in the port area:

🦐 **Dois de Fevereiro** (Sacadura Cabral, 79) — shrimp bobó and moqueca by chef João Diamante.
🥘 **G&G Gourmet** (Sacadura Cabral, 55) — Little Africa's famous shrimp bobó.
🐠 **Beco das Sardinhas / Bar Ocidental** (Miguel Couto, 139) — fresh sardines fried to order.
🍽️ **Trapiche Gamboa** (Sacadura Cabral, 155) and **Restaurante Mauá** (MAR rooftop) also serve seafood dishes.

🚊 How to get there: Parada dos Navios–Valongo station (Line 1).
💡 Tip: go at lunch to enjoy the best of the seafood catch.
comida-tipica-carioca-porto:
🇧🇷 Typical carioca food in the port area:

🍲 **Angu do Gomes** (Sacadura Cabral, 75) — creamy angu with offal, since 1955.
🫘 **Gracioso** (Sacadura Cabral, 97) — feijoada on Thursdays and Filé à Oswaldo Aranha.
🥁 **Botequim da Cidade do Samba** (Rivadávia Corrêa, 60 – Gamboa) — carioca snacks next to the samba schools.
🍛 **Imperatriz Gourmet** (Sacadura Cabral, 157) — a varied pay-by-weight buffet — and **Rio Park** (Sacadura Cabral, 67), Brazilian with 45+ years.

🚊 How to get there: Parada dos Navios–Valongo station (Line 1).
💡 Tip: Thursday is feijoada day at several of the area's bars!
restaurante-com-vista-porto:
🌇 Where to eat with a view in the port area:

🏙️ **Restaurante Mauá** — the rooftop (6th floor) of MAR, at Praça Mauá, with a view of the Museum of Tomorrow and the bay. Tue–Sun 12pm–6pm.
🍺 **Boteco Belmonte** — the air-conditioned terrace of the Touring Building (Praça Mauá), with a view of the Rio–Niterói Bridge.
🌅 **Bar do Omar** — atop the Morro do Pinto (Santo Cristo), with a view of the bay, the Bridge and Christ the Redeemer.
🎶 **Trapiche Gamboa** — an 1867 townhouse with a port atmosphere.

🚊 How to get there: Parada dos Museus station (Line 1), at Praça Mauá.
💡 Tip: go in the late afternoon for the sunset over the bay.
comida-saudavel-porto:
🥗 Healthy, light food in the area:

🌱 **Verde Vício** (Rua Buenos Aires, 22 – Downtown) — gourmet healthy food since 1996, the menu changes every day, with vegan and gluten-free options.
🍃 **Florescer Bistrô** and **Laurence Gastronomia** — at **Portinho** (Av. Rodrigues Alves, 135 – Saúde), with heartfelt dishes, salads and a historic setting.

🚊 How to get there: Verde Vício — Uruguaiana subway; Portinho — Parada dos Museus station (Line 1).
💡 Tip: great for a light lunch while touring the port area.
cafe-confeitaria-porto:
☕ Coffee and patisserie in the area:

🍰 **Confeitaria Colombo** (Rua Gonçalves Dias, 32 – Downtown) — a Belle Époque jewel (1894). Try the dulce de leche mille-feuille and the traditional afternoon tea.
🍝 **Café Tero** (Ladeira Felipe Néri, 11 – Morro da Conceição) — a charming Italian-Brazilian cantina, good for breakfast or a light lunch.

🚊 How to get there: Colombo — Carioca subway/tram; Café Tero — Harmonia station (Line 1).
💡 Tip: the Colombo usually has a line on weekends — arrive early.
comida-nordestina-porto:
🌵 Regional and northeastern food near the port:

🍲 **Imaculada Bar e Galeria** (Ladeira João Homem – Morro da Conceição) — bobó, feijoada and bean broth with shrimp, in an old-townhouse atmosphere.

And as a bonus near the port area:
🎪 **São Cristóvão Fair (Luiz Gonzaga Center of Northeastern Traditions)** — a stronghold of northeastern culture in Rio, with sun-dried beef, baião de dois and forró (in São Cristóvão, a few minutes from the port).

🚊 How to get there: Imaculada — Harmonia station (Line 1).
💡 Tip: the São Cristóvão Fair buzzes on weekends, with live music.
o-que-fazer-a-noite-porto:
🌙 At night, the port area comes alive, mainly with samba:

🎶 **Pedra do Sal** — historic samba circles (Mondays and Fridays), with the **Boteco Pedra do Sal** next door for snacks.
🥁 **Trapiche Gamboa** (Sacadura Cabral, 155) — live samba Wednesday, Friday and Saturday, in an 1867 townhouse.
🍺 **Gracioso** and the bars of Largo da Prainha — draft beer and snacks with a bohemian vibe.
🌇 **Boteco Belmonte** (Praça Mauá) — happy hour with a bay view; **Bar do Omar** (Morro do Pinto) — sunset and samba.
⛰️ **Imaculada** (Morro da Conceição) — bar, music and gallery.

🚊 How to get there: Parada dos Navios–Valongo, Parada dos Museus and Harmonia stations (Line 1).
💡 Tip: at night, prefer the busy areas (Pedra do Sal, Largo da Prainha) and use the tram/ride apps to get back.
boulevard-olimpico-info:
🌅 The **Olympic Boulevard** is the port-area waterfront revitalized for the 2016 Games — the **Orla Conde** promenade, about 3.5 km long, from **Praça Mauá** to Praça XV, in place of the old Perimetral elevated highway.

🎨 Highlights: the **Etnias Mural**, by Eduardo Kobra (five faces, one per continent — a world graffiti record), the **Museum of Tomorrow**, **MAR**, **AquaRio** and the **RioStar Ferris Wheel**.

🚊 How to get there: **Parada dos Museus** tram (VLT) station (Line 1), at Praça Mauá.
💡 Tip: it's a public, free area — walk it in the late afternoon for the sunset over the bay.
roda-gigante-riostar-info:
🎡 The **RioStar Ferris Wheel** (Praça Mauá) is said to be the **largest in Latin America**, at **88 m** (~25 floors). Opened on **6 December 2019**, it has **54 air-conditioned cabins** (up to 6 people) and the ride lasts about **20 minutes**.

👀 From the cabin you can see Guanabara Bay, the Rio–Niterói Bridge, the Museum of Tomorrow, Christ the Redeemer and Sugarloaf.

🎟️ Paid ticket (around R$ 69.90 — check the current price). ⏰ Generally 10am to 6pm (may vary).
🚊 How to get there: **Parada dos Museus** tram (VLT) station (Line 1), at Praça Mauá.
💡 Tip: go in the late afternoon for the sunset.
